commit.message:
[agento] fix: ensure terminal sessions are reaped by lifecycle-manager (orch-v7oa) (#397)

* [agento] fix: ensure terminal sessions are reaped by lifecycle-manager (orch-v7oa)

- Modified pollAll filter to include terminal sessions until they are removed from the list.\n- Removed transition guard for terminal cleanup in checkSession, ensuring kill() is retried if it fails.\n- This prevents zombie tmux sessions from accumulating when the initial cleanup call fails or is interrupted.

* [agento] fix(core): keep terminal cleanup retrying

* [agento] fix: keep terminal session reaping in the lifecycle poll path

* [agento] fix: normalize project paths when loading terminal sessions

* [agento] fix: address CR review comments on terminal session reaping

- Move anti-dedup mutation AFTER validateAndEmitExitProof succeeds so
  retries can re-emit if the first attempt threw
- Move reapPostMergeCoWorkers before sessionManager.kill so failed
  reaps are retryable (kill() archives the session, making later
  reaps unreachable if reap fails after kill)
- Replace unconditional sessionsToCheck filter with real gating:
  non-terminal sessions always poll; terminal sessions only poll if
  exit proof has not yet been recorded — avoids SCM/probe spam on
  settled terminal sessions
- Fix regression test to use fresh session reads from disk rather
  than returning the same mutated in-memory object

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [agento] fix(core): persist exit proof key only after successful kill to allow retry

* [agento] fix: add missing terminalExitProofRecordedAt persistence assertion

* [agento] fix: keep terminal exit proof retries idempotent

* [agento] chore: override vulnerable transitive deps

* [agento] test: harden stale worktree recovery mock

* [agento] fix: harden terminal session cleanup

* [agento] fix: defer cleanup until exit proof succeeds

* [agento] fix: harden terminal exit retries

* [agento] test: cover poll rediscovery and align spawn mocks

* [agento] fix: persist pending terminal exit proof markers

* [agento] fix: address remaining PR 397 review items

commit.message:
Add interactive "Impact & Influence" page (#3)

* Add interactive Impact & Influence page with D3.js visualizations

- Create impact.html with interactive citation network (D3.js force-directed graph)
  with three layout modes: force, radial, and clustered by field
- Add world map visualization showing global citation reach across 42 countries
  using TopoJSON and D3 geo projections
- Add animated disciplinary influence bar chart showing citation distribution
  across 12 research fields
- Add key impact metrics with animated counters (citations, countries,
  institutions, disciplines)
- Add interdisciplinary bridging section highlighting cross-field connections
- Add navigation link to Impact page from index.html header
- Match existing site design: dark theme, Inter font, teal/cyan/blue/violet
  color palette, glassmorphism cards, scroll-triggered animations
- Fully responsive across desktop, tablet, and mobile breakpoints

Co-authored-by: Evan Rees <evanroyrees@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review issues: mobile nav, deterministic data, XSS-safe tooltips

- Fix mobile nav overflow by adding flex-wrap at 768px breakpoint
  (both impact.html and index.html)
- Replace Math.random() with seeded PRNG (mulberry32) so the citation
  network visualization is deterministic across page reloads
- Replace innerHTML with DOM creation (textContent) in network and
  map tooltips to prevent potential XSS
- Fix fragile pulse ring SVG animation by appending both <animate>
  elements to the same circle reference instead of using :last-child
- Add meta description for SEO
- Remove unused data-suffix attributes from metric cards

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace fabricated impact data with real OpenAlex citation data

- Add scripts/fetch_impact_data.py: queries OpenAlex API by researcher
  ORCID to fetch real publications, citing works, geographic reach,
  and disciplinary influence data
- Add scripts/researchers.json: config with verified work IDs for
  Ian J. Miller and Evan Roy Rees (handles OpenAlex profile conflation)
- Add data/impact-data.json: generated output with real metrics
  (2,439 citations, 76 countries, 3,274 institutions, 22 disciplines)
- Update impact.html to load from JSON data file instead of hardcoded
  fake data; remove seeded PRNG and generateNetworkData()
- To add a new researcher: add their ORCID to researchers.json and
  re-run python3 scripts/fetch_impact_data.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address 8 review items: section reorder, real institution coords, UX polish

1. Remove duplicate COVID paper (preprint W3043425224 vs published W3091839106)
2. Move Global Reach section above Citation Network; use institution-level
   coordinates from OpenAlex ROR data (150 institutions with real lat/lon)
   instead of country centroids — tooltips now show institution names
3. Increase edge visibility in both Citation Network (0.06 -> 0.15 opacity)
   and Global Reach (0.08 -> 0.15 opacity connection lines)
4. Constrain Force Layout to canvas bounds with clamping in tick handler
   and centering forces so all nodes stay visible
5. Add staggered pulse animations to top-cited institution dots on the
   Global Reach map (similar to landing page edge pulse effect)
6. Aggregate disciplines with <5 citations into "Other Disciplines" row
7. Apply gradient to both "Impact" and "Influence" in the hero title
8. Add section navigation buttons (Global Reach, Citation Network,
   Disciplines) between hero and metrics for quick jumping

Also fixes container width detection (was returning 0/2px when async
code ran before layout) with minimum threshold fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Second round of review fixes: landing page CTA, UX polish, favicon

1. Add "Research That Travels" impact banner to landing page between
   Services and Team sections with CTA linking to impact.html
2. Add flowing pulse animations to Global Reach connection lines
   using animated stroke-dashoffset on SVG lines
3. Fix citation network aspect ratio: use fixed 900x700 simulation
   space instead of container dimensions to prevent flattening
4. Raise discipline aggregation threshold to ≤10 citations; add
   info icon (ⓘ) with hover tooltip showing aggregated disciplines
5. Use distinct gradients for "Impact" (teal→violet) and
   "Influence" (violet→teal) in hero title
6. Add SVG favicon (Phyla dot grid) to both impact.html and index.html
7. Change tooltip text from "Phyla Publication" to "Phyla Tech Publication"

Also fixes counter animation to use setTimeout-based loop instead of
requestAnimationFrame for better compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove hard node clamping from citation network layout

The Math.max/Math.min clamping in the tick handler was creating a
visible rectangular bounding box where nodes piled up at edges.
Replace with softer centering forces (forceX/forceY at 0.07) and
reduced charge repulsion (-60) so nodes cluster organically toward
the center without artificial boundary artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Improve installation admin view: organization/user lists and fix impersonation scrollbar bug (#4080) ## Summary Fixes #4079 Improves the installation admin views for organizations and accounts, and fixes the impersonation scrollbar bug. ## Changes ### Admin list improvements (organizations & users) - **Organizations list**: Replaced the card/grid layout with a table layout matching the existing accounts view for better scannability - **Accounts list**: Added creation date column and sorting support for consistency - **Creation dates**: Both organization and account lists now display `created_at` dates - **Sorting**: Added clickable column headers with sort direction indicators; defaults to newest first (`created_at DESC`) - **Missing names**: Organizations and accounts with empty names now show a truncated ID fallback instead of blank cells ### Backend changes - Added `CreatedAt`/`UpdatedAt` fields to the `Account` model (columns already exist in DB) - Added `created_at` to both admin list API responses (`/admin/api/organizations`, `/admin/api/accounts`) - Added `sort_by` and `sort_direction` query parameters to both endpoints - Created a reusable `resolveOrderClause` helper with allowlist-based column validation to prevent SQL injection - Changed default sort order from `name ASC` to `created_at DESC` (newest first) ### Impersonation scrollbar fix - Made the impersonation banner `sticky` at the top of the viewport so it doesn't push content below the fold or cause scrollbar issues ## Files changed - `pkg/models/account.go` — Added `CreatedAt`/`UpdatedAt` to Account; updated `ListAccounts` signature for sorting - `pkg/models/organization.go` — Updated `ListAllOrganizations` signature for sorting - `pkg/models/sorting.go` — New reusable `resolveOrderClause` helper - `pkg/public/admin.go` — Added `parseSorting`, `created_at` to responses, sorting support - `pkg/models/account_installation_admin_test.go` — Updated test calls for new function signatures - `web_src/src/pages/admin/OrganizationsList.tsx` — Converted from card grid to table with sorting - `web_src/src/pages/admin/AccountsList.tsx` — Added sorting and created_at column - `web_src/src/pages/admin/AccountRow.tsx` — Added created_at cell and missing name fallback - `web_src/src/components/ImpersonationBanner.tsx` — Fixed scrollbar bug with sticky positioning

commit.message:
Windows hip-tests: Run twice - PAL (pass/fail) and ROCR (informational) (#3593)

**Summary**  
hip-tests on Windows run twice for CI: once with **PAL** (pass/fail) and
once with **ROCR** (informational), controlled by `GPU_ENABLE_PAL=1` and
`0` as described in #3587.

**Technical Details**
- **Single job, matrix-driven:** When `test_artifacts` is run; the test
matrix is extended in `fetch_test_configurations.py` so that on Windows
the hip-tests component becomes two entries: **hip-tests (PAL)** and
**hip-tests (ROCR)**.
- **PAL:** `gpu_enable_pal: "1"`, no `expect_failure`, job result is
used for pass/fail.
- **ROCR:** `gpu_enable_pal: "0"`, `expect_failure: true`, same job type
but `continue-on-error`, so failures are informational.
- `GPU_ENABLE_PAL` is set in the Test step from the component (matrix).
Only the two hip-tests entries set it; other components leave it empty,
as it does not affect their execution. In the future, OpenCL tests will
also read this.

**Future**  
When ROCR is ready for pass/fail, swap which entry has `expect_failure`
(and optionally job names) in `fetch_test_configurations.py`.

commit.message:
Fix DM reactions popup drifting off-screen on scroll (#14020) ## Summary - Fixes a UI positioning bug where the DM reactions popup could drift upward/off-screen after opening. - Updates Harmony popup scroll tracking to clamp computed top position to viewport bounds when auto-flip/clamping is enabled. ## Root Cause - `Popup` initially clamps its position when opened, but the scroll listener later recomputed `top` without clamping. - In scrollable chat containers this allowed the popup to keep moving upward and leave the viewport. ## Changes - `packages/harmony/src/components/popup/Popup.tsx` - In `watchScroll`, compute `nextTop` and clamp to `[0, window.innerHeight - popupHeight]` unless `disableAutoFlip` is enabled. - Add `disableAutoFlip` to callback deps. ## Validation - Manual code-path validation of popup positioning logic. - Attempted targeted lint command, but local workspace tools were unavailable (`eslint: not found`) in this environment.
  
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Ray Jacobson

commit.message:
Fix duplicate upload notifications for collection track publishes (#14031) ## Summary - Ensure collection (playlist/album) child tracks are explicitly marked as playlist uploads before publish. - Preserve `is_playlist_upload` through web upload metadata adaptation and SDK upload schema parsing. - Prevent backend `create` track notifications from fanning out for each child track in collection upload retries. ## Changes - `packages/common/src/api/tan-query/upload/usePublishCollection.ts`: set `metadata.is_playlist_upload = true` when composing child track metadata for collection uploads. - `packages/common/src/adapters/track.ts`: include `is_playlist_upload` in `trackMetadataForUploadToSdk()` output. - `packages/sdk/src/sdk/api/tracks/types.ts`: add `isPlaylistUpload` to `UploadTrackMetadataSchema` so strict parsing keeps the field. ## Notes - Root cause traced to missing propagation of `is_playlist_upload`; discovery-provider trigger logic only suppresses per-track create notifications when this field is true. - Targeted tests were not runnable in this environment because `vitest` is not available in the package runtime path.
  
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Ray Jacobson

commit.message:
Fix track overflow menu showing delete action in playlist edit context (#14098) ## Summary - hide `Delete Track` from the track overflow menu when the menu is being used in collection page remove-flow context (`onRemove` present) - keep `Delete Track` available in other contexts ## Root Cause - the table overflow menu for collection pages injects a contextual "Remove from this playlist/album" item, but still relied on `TrackMenu` default `includeDelete=true` - this caused owners to see both remove and delete in the same menu, making accidental destructive deletes possible ## Testing - attempted focused web test run: `npm run -w packages/web test -- --runInBand OverflowMenuButton` - environment limitation: `vitest: not found` in this workspace

commit.message:
Mobile: fix /playlists/:id deep link routing (#14092) ### What’s going on On mobile, the deep-link handler was treating opaque playlist ID URLs like `/playlists/:id` as **profile** links. It navigated to the `Profile` screen with `id` set to the playlist id, which leaves the profile page stuck showing its skeleton. ### Fix - Route `/playlists/:id` to the `Collection` screen with `{ id }`. - Extracted the deeplink path parsing into a small utility so it can be unit-tested. ### Tests - Added a focused Jest test covering `/users/:id` → `Profile` and `/playlists/:id` → `Collection`. - Run with: `npx jest -c packages/mobile/jest.deeplink.config.js`

## Summary

- **Plaid**: Optional `PLAID_API_HOST` (`sandbox` | `production`),
trimmed client id/secret, and Plaid `Configuration` using `apiKey` with
the correct base path. Link token creation maps Plaid HTTP errors to
`createError`, logs failures, and only passes a webhook URL when it
resolves to HTTPS.
- **Env**: Server loads the repo root `.env` first, then `app/.env`
(override), so `pnpm dev` from the monorepo root still picks up shared
variables.
- **UI**: Sync accounts uses `useAppFetch` with clearer errors,
`ClientOnly` + dynamic `PlaidLink` for Pug SFC resolution, a table
`:key` for synced rows, and disconnect flow improvements.
- **Tooling**: `predev` runs `prisma generate`; Vite `optimizeDeps`
includes `@jcss/vue-plaid-link`; `.env.example` documents
`PLAID_API_HOST`.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it changes Plaid environment selection, server env
loading order, and Plaid link-token error mapping/logging, which can
affect production integrations and startup behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Plaid integration is now configurable and more robust.** Adds
optional `PLAID_API_HOST` (with `.env.example` docs) and trims Plaid
credentials in `envSchema`, updates the Plaid client configuration to
pick the base host via `PLAID_API_HOST`/`DEPLOY_ENV` and provide headers
via `apiKey`.
> 
> **Improves Plaid link-token creation and UI sync flows.** `POST
/api/plaid-link` now only sends a webhook when it resolves to an HTTPS
URL and maps Plaid HTTP failures to `createError` with structured
logging/health recording. The `SyncAccountsTab` switches to
`useAppFetch`, standardizes error messaging, forces table rerender via a
computed `:key`, fixes PlaidLink usage in Pug via `ClientOnly` + dynamic
`:is`, and makes disconnect update the UI immediately before reloading
server state.
> 
> **Developer/tooling tweaks.** Server env loading now reads repo-root
`.env` then `app/.env` (override) for monorepo runs, Vite pre-bundles
`@jcss/vue-plaid-link`, and `predev` runs `prisma generate`.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit
7e263ddfac5fe1b9cf596bb151ef91255efb5464. Configure
[here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

* Fix: CSS class names `.portal-kpi-label`, `.portal-kpi-value`, and `.portal-kpi-sub` don't match the BEM-style class names used in React components (`portal-kpi-card__label`, `portal-kpi-card__value`, `portal-kpi-card__hint`), causing KPI card elements to have no styling.


This commit fixes the issue reported at components/portal/PortalKpiCard.tsx:35

## Bug Analysis

The issue is a mismatch between CSS class names in `css/main.css` and the class names used in React components (`components/portal/PortalKpiCard.tsx` and `app/(portal)/employer/page.tsx`).

**What was wrong:**

*   CSS defined: `.portal-kpi-label`, `.portal-kpi-value`, `.portal-kpi-sub`
*   Components used: `portal-kpi-card__label`, `portal-kpi-card__value`, `portal-kpi-card__hint`

This means the label, value, and hint elements inside KPI cards have no styling applied to them - they don't get the intended:

*   Font sizes (11px for labels, 1.875rem for values, 0.75rem for hints)
*   Font weights (800 for labels, 900 for values)
*   Text transforms (uppercase for labels)
*   Letter spacing
*   Colors (var(--color-on-surface-variant) for labels/hints)
*   Margins

The KPI cards appear in the employer portal dashboard to show metrics like open positions, total applications, etc. Without these styles, the cards would look broken/unstyled.

## The Fix

Updated `css/main.css` to use the BEM-style class names that match the React components:

*   `.portal-kpi-label` → `.portal-kpi-card__label`
*   `.portal-kpi-value` → `.portal-kpi-card__value`
*   `.portal-kpi-sub` → `.portal-kpi-card__hint`

The old CSS class names were not used anywhere else in the codebase (verified via grep), so updating the CSS is the safer and cleaner approach. It also brings the CSS into alignment with BEM naming conventions which is more maintainable.


Co-authored-by: Vercel <vercel[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mabrown040 <mabrown040@gmail.com>

* Fix: CSS class `portal-pad-x` is used in 7 places across partner/page.tsx and counselor/page.tsx but never defined in CSS


This commit fixes the issue reported at app/(portal)/partner/page.tsx:178

**Bug Analysis:**

The issue occurs because the codebase uses the class name `portal-pad-x` in 7 locations across two files:

*   `app/(portal)/partner/page.tsx` (2 occurrences, lines 178 and 193)
*   `app/(portal)/counselor/page.tsx` (5 occurrences, lines 100, 106, 120, 140, 148)

However, the CSS file `css/main.css` only defines `.portal-pad` (which sets horizontal padding) but never defines `.portal-pad-x`. As a result, all elements using `className="portal-pad-x"` receive no horizontal padding, breaking the intended layout and causing inconsistent spacing across the portal pages.

This is clearly a bug - likely the developer either:

1.  Meant to use `.portal-pad` but typed `.portal-pad-x`
2.  Intended to create `.portal-pad-x` but forgot to add it to the CSS

Given that `.portal-pad-x` is used consistently across multiple files and components, option 2 seems more likely - the CSS definition was simply forgotten.

**Fix:**

Added the missing `.portal-pad-x` CSS class definition to `css/main.css`, placed immediately after the `.portal-pad` class for consistency. The new class applies the same horizontal padding (`padding-left: 1.5rem; padding-right: 1.5rem;`) that was clearly intended based on the existing `.portal-pad` class and the purpose described in the CSS comment "Portal layout utilities (uniform, Apple-like spacing)".

commit.message:
Fix/dex oneinch lop passthrough own trades (#9552)

* fix(dex): passthrough 1inch LOP from own_trades; avoid enrich on order tokens

1inch limit-order-protocol rows in oneinch_lop_own_trades use order-level token_bought/sold addresses and amounts, while the executed swap can involve different assets. Sending those rows through enrich_dex_trades applied ERC20 decimals (and downstream USD math) using the order addresses, which mis-scaled normalized amounts and inflated amount_usd.

- Remove per-chain oneinch_lop_*_base_trades from dex_*_base_trades unions.

- Add oneinch_lop_dex_trades_passthrough macro and union its output into each dex_*_trades so the 1inch LOP leg matches ref('oneinch_lop_own_trades') as-is.

The macro was missing an is_incremental() check with incremental_predicate,
causing a full table scan of the oneinch_lop_own_trades view on every
incremental run across all 13 chain-level dex_*_trades models. This adds the
same dev_dates/incremental guard pattern used by dex_base_trades_macro and

commit.message:
feat(staged-agent): implement v0 DAG-scheduled agent runtime (#11) ## Summary First iteration of the staged-agent runtime per `pi/packages/staged-agent/STAGE_RUNTIME_PLAN.md`. A Spark-aligned DAG-scheduled agent runtime using Pi sessions as the execution primitive, with an **actor-based concurrency model**, **message-driven timeouts**, and **native Pi session runtime integration with end-to-end tests**. ## Architecture ### Actor hierarchy ``` JobRunner (entry point, not an actor) └── DAGSchedulerActor (central orchestrator) ├── StageActor (one per stage attempt) │ └── TaskActor (one per task attempt) └── ... parallel stages PiSessionPool (wraps AgentSessionRuntime) ``` ### Components | Component | File | Role | |-----------|------|------| | **Actor** | `actor.ts` | Base class: mailbox, drain, `sendDelayed`/`cancelDelayed`, lifecycle hooks | | **Types** | `types.ts` | Job, Stage, Task, TaskSet, TaskResult, CompletionPolicy, TransitionFn | | **Events** | `events.ts` | 17-variant `RuntimeEvent` union with `seq` | | **MutableDAG** | `dag.ts` | DAG with `resetStage()`, `pause()`, self-loop rejection | | **EventLog** | `event-log.ts` | NDJSON: fsync, append-after-close guard, corrupt-line-safe replay, seq numbers | | **State** | `state.ts` | Left-fold projection: dynamic stages, reset, pause/resume, stageResults | | **SessionPoolActor** | `session-pool-actor.ts` | In-memory pool for unit testing | | **PiSessionPool** | `pi-runtime.ts` | **Wraps `AgentSessionRuntime`** — creates/disposes real Pi sessions with concurrency and `parentSession` lineage | | **PiTaskExecutor** | `pi-runtime.ts` | **Drives real `AgentSession`** — sends `prompt()`, subscribes to events, awaits `agent_end`, extracts result | | **PiExecutor (CLI)** | `pi-executor.ts` | Spawns `pi --print` for lightweight CLI-based execution | | **TaskActor** | `task-actor.ts` | Message-driven state machine. AbortSignal to executor. Timeouts via sendDelayed. | | **StageActor** | `stage-actor.ts` | Completion policies, retries, timeouts, empty-stage handling | | **DAGSchedulerActor** | `dag-scheduler-actor.ts` | DAG scheduling, transitions, failDependents (BFS), pause/resume, inspection | | **JobRunner** | `job-runner.ts` | run, cancel, resume, inspect, recover | | **bin/pi** | `bin/pi` | DotSlash file for pi CLI v0.66.1 | ### Native Pi integration `PiSessionPool` wraps the real `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` SDK: - **Creates** `AgentSessionRuntime` instances via `createAgentSessionRuntime` + `createAgentSessionServices` + `createAgentSessionFromServices` - **Session lineage** via `runtime.newSession({ parentSession })` for task attempt lineage - **Concurrency** with back-pressure queuing - **Dispose** tears down runtimes cleanly `createPiTaskExecutor` drives sessions natively: 1. Acquires a `PiSession` from the pool 2. Sends the task prompt via `session.prompt()` 3. Subscribes to `AgentSessionEvent`, waits for `agent_end` 4. Extracts `getLastAssistantText()` + session metadata (file, id, model, usage) into `TaskResult.signals` 5. Supports `AbortSignal` (calls `session.abort()`) 6. Releases session back to pool Session transcripts, tool usage, and lineage persist in Pi's native JSONL storage — the runtime stays thin. ## Tests 82 tests across 28 suites, all passing: ### Unit tests (73) | Suite | Tests | |-------|-------| | Actor | 4 | | Actor — sendDelayed | 3 | | Actor — lifecycle hooks | 4 | | Deferred | 2 | | MutableDAG | 13 | | EventLog | 10 | | JobRunner core | 15 | | Pause/resume | 1 | | Review loop | 1 | | Inspection | 1 | | Recovery | 2 | | Task timeout | 2 | | Acquire timeout | 1 | | Empty stage | 1 | | Transition throws | 1 | | AbortSignal | 2 | | stageResults reset | 1 | | SessionPoolActor | 3 | | projectState | 7 | ### CLI integration tests (5) — pi subprocess | Suite | What it tests | |-------|---------------| | Single stage | Prompt → JSON response | | Linear chain | plan → implement | | Parallel stages | Fan-out → fan-in | | Transition | Dynamic finalize stage | | Event log | Replay + projectState | ### Native Pi session tests (4) — real AgentSessionRuntime | Suite | What it tests | |-------|---------------| | Single stage | Prompt → response + session metadata (sessionFile, sessionId) | | Linear chain | plan → implement via real sessions | | Event log | session_attached events + replay | | Transition | Dynamic stage materialization |
  
--------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Brian Hart

commit.message:
Fix brittle APIError assertion in langfuse middleware test (#407) ## Summary - update `ai/src/langfuseRoutesMiddleware.test.ts` to stop using `toBeInstanceOf(APIError)` for the forbidden-path assertion - assert on stable APIError fields (`name`, `status`, `title`, `disableExternalErrorTracking`) instead - preserve the existing behavior check that `next` is not called when access is forbidden ## Why The CI failure was caused by a constructor identity mismatch between the imported `APIError` symbol and the thrown `APIError` instance in this workspace/compiled setup. The middleware behavior was correct, but the `instanceof` assertion was brittle. ## Validation - `bun run --filter @terreno/ai lint` - `bun run --filter @terreno/ai compile` - `bun test ai/src/langfuseRoutesMiddleware.test.ts` - `bun run compile` (workspace-wide) Note: workspace-wide lint currently has unrelated pre-existing failures outside this change (e.g. `@terreno/example-frontend` formatting).
  
--- > [!NOTE] > **Low Risk** > Low risk: changes only a unit test assertion to avoid brittle `instanceof` checks; production middleware behavior is untouched. > > **Overview** > Updates `langfuseRoutesMiddleware.test.ts` to stop importing `APIError` and replaces the brittle `toBeInstanceOf(APIError)` check with assertions on stable error properties (`name`, `status`, `title`, `disableExternalErrorTracking`) while keeping the existing expectation that `next` is not called on forbidden access. > > Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit ddfc33f89ac8a7ecd585b5d0a9db1158d8fe4cb0. Admin Plaid sync logs: model, UI, API, and sync instrumentation (#57)

Adds a `PlaidSyncLog` Prisma model and migration, then wires admin
tooling and runtime logging.

**Includes:** admin `/admin/plaid-sync-logs` page and nav entry, `GET
/api/admin/plaid-sync-logs`, `recordPlaidSyncLog` helper, and expanded
`PlaidSyncService` instrumentation so sync attempts are persisted for
review. Zod schemas and field-encryption reencrypt stubs are updated for
the new model.

**Commits:** migration + schema in an isolated commit (repo hook);
feature commit; small ESLint fix for `ProgressReportCallback` type
imports in reencrypt files.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds persistent DB writes and new error-handling paths inside
`PlaidSyncService` sync flows, which could impact sync performance and
failure behavior if logging/migrations misbehave. Admin UI/API changes
are straightforward but depend on the new `plaid_sync_log` table being
present and sized appropriately.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Adds end-to-end Plaid sync run logging and an admin viewer.** A new
`PlaidSyncLog` Prisma model + migration introduces a `plaid_sync_log`
table capturing sync mode/status, item/user, timings, transaction
counts, errors, and JSON metadata.
> 
> **Exposes logs to admins.** Adds `GET /api/admin/plaid-sync-logs`
(with Zod-validated filters/pagination) and a new
`/admin/plaid-sync-logs` page (filters, expandable metadata, load-more
pagination), plus a nav entry in `AdminPageShell`.
> 
> **Instruments sync execution paths.** `PlaidSyncService` now records
`success`/`partial`/`failed` log entries for both cursor-based item sync
and legacy token batch sync, including capturing per-transaction
processing errors and summary stats.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit
c4ad4b03162bf65b888c8439374e6ad648c995b6. Configure
[here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

fix: install bundled cheats and app-mappings from AppImage resources (#1)

Tauri v2 places resources that use ../ under subfolders of resource_dir
(e.g. _up_/bundled-cheats/). First-run setup only listed the resource root,
so packaged builds copied nothing. Copy bundled .md and app-mappings.yaml
recursively from resource_dir, and map bundled-cheats/ to the bundle root.

Add GKE CronJob and CI support for database key rotation (#56)

This change adds a Kubernetes CronJob template for scheduled database
key rotation plus a `k8s-rotate-db-key.sh` helper in the microservice
package.

The microservice Dockerfile and `package.json` are extended so CI can
build an image that includes the rotation tooling. The build-push GCR
action, `deploy-dineros-gke.sh`, and both `main` and `pr` workflows are
wired so that path can be built and deployed consistently. The dineros
deploy template is adjusted to align with the new CronJob, and staging
profile gets a small env addition. Lockfile updated for the new
microservice dependency.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces automated DB field-encryption key rotation that patches
Kubernetes secrets and restarts deployments, so misconfiguration could
break decryption or cause staging downtime. Scope is mostly staging-only
infra/CI changes, but it touches sensitive key management workflows.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a **staging-only** GKE CronJob (with scoped RBAC + service
account) to perform nightly DB field-encryption key rotation, restart
the main + microservice deployments, and trigger a microservice `GET
/migrate` to re-encrypt data.
> 
> Updates the deploy template to source DB encryption keys via `envFrom`
from `${SECRET_DB}` (instead of enumerating individual key env vars) and
clarifies that `${SECRET_DB}` must not contain `DATABASE_URL`. CI/CD is
extended to build/push a separate `-keyrotate` microservice image via a
new Docker build `target` and to apply the CronJob during staging
deploys; the microservice image gains a `runner-keyrotate` target, the
`k8s-rotate-db-key.sh` helper, and the `@47ng/cloak` dependency for key
generation.

fix: resolve channel-core.js by path instead of require('openclaw/plugin-sdk') (#8)

require('openclaw/plugin-sdk') returns the compat proxy from
root-alias.cjs, which does NOT export channel-core functions like
createChannelPluginBase or defineChannelPluginEntry.

New approach: use require.resolve('openclaw/plugin-sdk') to find the
physical location of the SDK directory, then require the sibling
channel-core.js file directly. This works regardless of the
installation layout (global, local, double node_modules).

fix(MarkdownEditor): fix `isElement` crash reading `undefined.children` when `editor.children` is directly reassigned (#472)

* fix(MarkdownEditor): 修复直接赋值 editor.children 导致 isElement 读取 undefined.children 崩溃

根因：多处直接赋值 editor.children 绕过 Slate 操作流，但未先清除
editor.selection，导致旧 selection 引用已不存在的节点路径。
当 React 重新渲染触发 toDOMRange → above → isElement 时，
Node.get 返回 undefined，isElement(undefined) 报错
'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading children)'。

修复方案：
- store.ts: 所有直接赋值 editor.children 前先置 selection = null
- editorUtils.ts: reset 方法同样先清除 selection
- Editor.tsx: toDOMRange 调用前校验 selection 路径有效性，增加 try-catch 兜底
- setContent: 移除对旧 children 的尾部换行插入逻辑，避免赋值后用旧引用操作

* fix(MarkdownEditor): 恢复 setContent 中尾部换行插入逻辑，修复 CI 测试失败

setContent 简化时误删了对旧 children 末尾文本节点追加 \n 的逻辑，
导致 store.test.ts 中 '当旧内容最后一项为无尾换行的文本节点时追加
insertText(\n)' 测试断言失败。

现在在清除 selection 和赋值 children 之前先保存旧 currentChildren
引用，赋值后仍执行原有的尾部换行检查。

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds new Trezor connection/readiness logic and broadens
hardware-wallet error parsing, which could affect how Trezor/Ledger
failures are classified and surfaced during signing flows. Main risk is
regressions in hardware-wallet connection state/race handling and error
mapping, but changes are mostly additive and covered by tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `TrezorAdapter` (not yet wired into the adapter factory)
that uses WebUSB presence checks plus `getTrezorFeatures` preflight
validation (unlock/initialized/session, required capabilities, and Model
One message-size limits).
> 
> Extends the Trezor offscreen pipeline by adding a
`trezor-get-features` action from UI/store → controller → offscreen
bridge → `@trezor/connect-web`, and bumps `@metamask/eth-trezor-keyring`
to `^9.1.0` with updated LavaMoat allowances.
> 
> Improves hardware-wallet UX/state handling by parameterizing “unlock”
recovery copy with the wallet type (new
`hardwareWalletErrorRecoveryConnection4` and localization updates),
adding `preflightMessageBytes` and better `ensureDeviceReady` dedup/race
tracking (`isEnsuringDeviceReadyRef`), and enhancing `rpcErrorUtils` to
recognize/map Trezor SDK string codes and user-cancel/reject text more
safely.
